title: Mixed Practice — Time, Mass, and Volume
essential_question: How can you choose the right unit and operation for time, mass, or volume problems?

60.note: Reading the minute hand as the number it points to

learn.title: Three Types of Measurement
learn.content: TIME = how long. MASS = how heavy. VOLUME = how much liquid. Each uses different units.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Time Recap
learn.content: Hour, minute. 1 hour = 60 minutes. Use elapsed time on a number line. Add hours and minutes; roll over at 60.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Mass Recap
learn.content: Gram (g), kilogram (kg). 1 kg = 1000 g. Use grams for small, kg for big. Use balance to measure.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Volume Recap
learn.content: Liter (L), milliliter (mL). 1 L = 1000 mL. Use mL for small, L for big.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Same Operations
learn.content: All three (time, mass, volume) use ADD, SUBTRACT, MULTIPLY, DIVIDE in word problems. Look for keywords: 'total', 'left', 'each', 'shared'.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Match Units
learn.content: Always check units. If problem mixes 1 kg and 200 g, convert before computing. 1 kg = 1000 g, so total = 1000 + 200 = 1200 g.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Estimate First
learn.content: Always estimate. A juice box is mL not L. A person is kg not g. A movie is hours, not seconds. Keep answers reasonable.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Lesson Summary
learn.content: Today: (1) Time, mass, volume — different units, similar operations. (2) Convert when units differ. (3) Estimate to check answers. (4) Use add/subtract/multiply/divide based on the problem.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
